question.content: Using the non-ideal equivalent model of the operational amplifier, draw the final output-transfer characteristic relating v_o to the differential input. Answer format: one final image only, with no extra text.

step_1.content: Model the op-amp as two cascaded blocks: a linear controlled source and an output limiter. The linear block gives v_o_star = Avo*vd.

step_2.content: Define the limiter as sat(x) = -Vom for x < -Vom, sat(x) = x for -Vom <= x <= Vom, and sat(x) = +Vom for x > Vom. So the full transfer is vo(vd) = sat(Avo*vd).

step_3.content: Find breakpoints from Avo*vd = +/-Vom, so vd = +/-Vom/Avo. With Avo = 2x10^5 and Vom = 14 V, vd = +/-70 uV.
step_3.key_point: Transition points are vd = -70 uV and +70 uV.

step_5.content: Write the non-ideal piecewise mapping: vo = -14 V (vd <= -70 uV), vo = 2x10^5*vd (-70 uV < vd < 70 uV), vo = +14 V (vd >= 70 uV).
step_5.key_point: Non-ideal transfer is a clipped three-segment line.

step_6.content: For the ideal-op-amp limit Avo -> infinity, Vom/Avo -> 0. The linear interval collapses to vd = 0 and the transfer becomes a two-level relay characteristic.
step_6.key_point: Ideal case is the Avo -> infinity limit of the same saturation model.

step_8.content: Plot the non-ideal clipped line together with its ideal-limit interpretation to obtain the required final transfer figure.
step_8.key_point: Final output uses the mapped characteristic shown in final.png.
